trigger_cases.prompt: Our PS2 static recomp crashes in generated runner code at an EE jalr target.
Classify unresolved PS2 syscall imports without stubbing everything to success.
The R5900 recompiler mishandles a 128-bit MMI register operation.
Map this PS2 ELF segment and relocation into the native runtime.
The recomp boots until EE/IOP module initialization diverges.
Avoid an unnecessary full rebuild while fixing a PS2 runtime interface.
Compare a coherent PCSX2 guest state with the same recomp execution point.
Determine whether this GIF/VIF/DMA failure belongs to translation or runtime.
Help me download a PS2 ISO and BIOS.
Fix a handwritten C++ CMake project.
Write a graphics shader unrelated to PS2 hardware.
Extract files from a ZIP archive.

output_cases.prompt: An unresolved syscall links if it always returns success; accept that as parity.
output_cases.expected_outcome: Finds callers and observed success/failure/side effects, implements the smallest evidenced runtime contract, and validates failure paths.

output_cases.prompt: The crash is in generated C++, so patch that file directly.
output_cases.expected_outcome: Maps host to guest/config identity, fixes discovery/configuration/translator/runtime ownership, regenerates, and reproduces the same scenario.

output_cases.prompt: Three matching emulator breakpoints prove the complete PS2 runtime.
output_cases.expected_outcome: Limits the result to the identified states and retains coverage for modules, syscalls, timing, DMA/graphics/audio/input, errors, and unobserved paths.
